title: Maximum Number of Hats to Buy

statement.markdown:
You are given $n$ people and their respective heights. Each person can buy at most one hat. You have $k$ different types of hats, of which the $i^{th}$ type is of size $a_i$. The cost of getting a hat of size $b$ to a person of height $h$ is $max(0, b-h)$. Your task is to maximize the number of people that can get a hat.

You need to write a function that takes an array of integers $a$, representing the sizes of the $k$ different types of hats, and an integer $n$, representing the number of people. Return the maximum number of people that can get a hat.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of different types of hats $k$. The next line contains $k$ integers $a_i$ representing the sizes of the $i^{th}$ type of hat. The last line contains a single integer $n$ representing the number of people.
statement.outputFormat: Return the maximum number of people that can get a hat.

examples.explanation: Maximum number of people that can get a hat is 2

hints.text: The greedy strategy is to maximize the number of people that can get a hat of size b to a person of height h. The cost of getting a hat of size b to a person of height h is max(0, b-h). To maximize the number of people that can get a hat, we should select the people with the maximum height first.

editorial.title: Maximum Number of Hats to Buy
editorial.intuition: The greedy strategy is to maximize the number of people that can get a hat of size b to a person of height h. The cost of getting a hat of size b to a person of height h is max(0, b-h). To maximize the number of people that can get a hat, we should select the people with the maximum height first.
editorial.bruteForce: Brute force approach: Try all possible combinations of people and hats to find the maximum number of people that can get a hat.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a greedy algorithm to maximize the number of people that can get a hat. The greedy strategy is to select the people with the maximum height first and assign the size of the hat to each person such that the cost of getting the hat to the person is minimized.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to sort the people and the hats in descending order and then greedily assign the size of the hat to each person such that the cost of getting the hat to the person is minimized.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the greedy algorithm can be proved by showing that the optimal solution has the same number of people as the greedy solution.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run: Sort the people and the hats in descending order and then greedily assign the size of the hat to each person such that the cost of getting the hat to the person is minimized.
commonMistakes: Not sorting the people and the hats in descending order
Not greedily assigning the size of the hat to each person

functionSignature.python: def maxHats(self, hats: List[int], n: int) -> int:
